title: Hymenolepis abbreviata
taxonomy: Hymenolepis abbreviata is a species of tapeworm. It belongs to the phylum Platyhelminthes and the class Cestoda. The order and family of Hymenolepis abbreviata are also Cestoda, with some sources classifying it as the family Hymenolepididae. This classification is based on the parasite's structure and its position within the platyhelminth tree. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of Hymenolepis abbreviata.

intro: Hymenolepis abbreviata, also known as Desert Lavender or Shortstem Hymenolepis, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to relieve congestion and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Hymenolepis abbreviata include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Desert Lavender is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also employed in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
